council.name: The Strategist
council.role: Big picture, revenue opportunities, market positioning

scope: Coda ERP (DILESA, ANSA, ANSA-Ventas, SR Group, RDB)

memos.title: Inaugural Council Session — Live Spine, RDB Integrity, and DILESA Sequencing
memos.summary: The inaugural council converged on a simple thesis: BSOP should become a live operating spine, but only through disciplined sequencing. The highest-confidence moves are to make AI Operations data live, harden RDB around Waitry instead of replacing it, and treat DILESA as the first formal modernization program while protecting Beto’s bandwidth during active deal and travel windows.

ideas.title: Convert BSOP from showcase to live operating spine
ideas.summary: Prioritize a live data spine for AI Operations and the most important operating surfaces so bsop.io becomes Beto’s daily control room instead of a manually refreshed presentation. Start with usage/session telemetry because the pipeline is bounded and already valuable, then reuse the same pattern for travel expenses and business dashboards.

ideas.title: Build the RDB ops core around Waitry instead of replacing the whole POS
ideas.summary: Keep the working QR experience in Waitry, stop trying to replace the entire front-of-house stack, and build the proprietary moat around reconciliations, deduplication, inventory, requisitions, receiving, warehouse control, and cash-cut workflows. This matches Beto’s strength: system architecture around real operations, not commodity POS hardware.

ideas.title: Treat DILESA as the flagship modernization program
ideas.summary: DILESA has the largest Coda surface area, the highest schema complexity, and the clearest long-term payoff if external data syncs eventually become real. Formalize it as a multi-wave modernization program: governance, god-table reduction, high-volume table strategy, and clearer portfolio stage control across land, anteproyectos, proyectos, and construction.

ideas.title: Use shared trip links as the first external-facing BSOP product
ideas.summary: The trip sharing + expense tracker already has a natural product shape: no-login links, itinerary visibility, maps, and settlement. It can become the first polished “other people touch BSOP” experience without exposing the rest of the operating system, and it fits Beto’s real travel behavior.

ideas.title: Ship a normalized telemetry pipeline for OpenClaw → BSOP
ideas.summary: Normalize session JSONL, manifests, and derived metrics into one stable schema, then drive the AI dashboard from that schema instead of hand-regenerated files. This creates a reusable ingestion architecture for every future BSOP module that starts life as files and later graduates into Supabase.

ideas.title: Install a deterministic dedupe and archiving layer for RDB orders
ideas.summary: Use the March evidence (1,127 orders, 95 suspicious duplicate pairs, 33 strong pairs) to build deterministic duplicate scoring and archival rules. Store canonical orders, flag probable duplicates, and archive old inbound rows so the large Coda table stops degrading performance.

ideas.title: Create a table-tiering policy for Coda-heavy systems
ideas.summary: Define which tables stay in Coda as workflow UI, which get archived periodically, and which should eventually sync with external storage. This is especially urgent for DILESA and RDB where table growth and dependency webs are starting to drag performance and maintainability.

ideas.title: Turn manual BSOP data refreshes into scheduled jobs with health checks
ideas.summary: The team already identified that static JSON erodes trust because it requires manual regeneration. Add scheduled collectors plus freshness markers, success/failure logging, and a visible “data updated at” contract so every dashboard states its own recency honestly.

ideas.title: Create an executive scoreboard across ANSA, DILESA, COAGAN, and RDB
ideas.summary: Build one weekly scoreboard with only the few KPIs that matter to Beto: cash/collections, project stage movement, inventory exceptions, duplicate-order incidents, open operational blockers, and upcoming commitments. The point is cross-business comparability, not exhaustive reporting.

ideas.title: Use passive overdue reminders as an accounts-follow-up queue
ideas.summary: Beto explicitly uses overdue reminders as passive follow-up, especially for pending income near month-end. Instead of treating them as clutter, operationalize them: monthly collection queue, owner, amount, age, expected close date, and unresolved reasons.

ideas.title: Use travel expense data as the first shared settlement analytics module
ideas.summary: The Baja import and shared-link flow already provide real expense data. Add per-person balance views, category splits, FX handling, and trip cost benchmarks so travel becomes the cleanest proof that BSOP can coordinate data among multiple humans without heavy auth.

ideas.title: Quantify the cost of operational friction before replacing systems
ideas.summary: Before any bigger rebuild, measure what RDB duplicate orders, Coda slowdown, and manual refresh work are actually costing in pesos, hours, and trust. This prevents prestige projects and helps sequence work by measurable pain, not by technical curiosity.

ideas.title: Do not launch three migrations at once
ideas.summary: BSOP live data, DILESA modernization, and RDB back-office hardening are all good ideas, but running them simultaneously would dilute attention and create half-built systems. Force a sequence: one proving ground, one adjacent build, one monitored backlog.

ideas.title: Avoid custom front-desk tools for non-technical staff until back-office is stable
ideas.summary: RDB has few employees and low technical tolerance. Any custom point-of-sale or front-desk workflow introduced too early will create support burden and resistance. Build invisible controls first, where the system protects the business without forcing new behavior changes on staff.

ideas.title: Use Supabase surgically, not as an excuse to rebuild everything
ideas.summary: Supabase is useful for shared travel and live metrics, but it can easily become a gravity well that turns every problem into a schema project. The council should treat Supabase as a selective substrate for high-friction workflows, not a religion.

ideas.title: Protect founder bandwidth during PED and Taos windows
ideas.summary: There is an active PED land negotiation and an imminent Taos family trip. The risk is loading Beto with platform design work exactly when live business and family commitments need attention. Recommendations should create leverage with low daily management overhead.

ideas.title: Make BSOP the wrapper, Coda the workflow UI, and Supabase the selective data spine
ideas.summary: The emerging architecture is already visible: Coda remains the highly adaptable operator interface, BSOP becomes the portfolio dashboard and shared surface, and Supabase is used only where live data, scale, or external sharing justify it. Naming this clearly reduces random architectural drift.

ideas.title: Institute a weekly council-driven operating review
ideas.summary: Tie the Sunday-night R&D Council to a Monday operating review artifact: top risks, top experiments, dashboards freshness, and what changed in each business. This converts the council from an inspiration engine into a real management rhythm.

ideas.title: Extend the DILESA land pipeline from offered parcels to owned inventory
ideas.summary: This honors Beto’s explicit preference that Terrenos cover both evaluated parcels and land already owned. It enables a single system of record for deal sourcing, evaluation history, ownership status, and future project conversion.

ideas.title: Adopt a data trust contract across all dashboards
ideas.summary: Every dashboard should declare source, freshness, owner, and whether the metric is live, scheduled, or manual. This small discipline protects credibility as BSOP expands from static pages into operational dashboards.

debate.topic: Opening positions — where is the highest leverage right now?

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: BSOP already looks credible. The gap is not design anymore; it is trust. If the data is stale, the platform stays a showroom instead of becoming Beto’s cockpit.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: AI Operations is the cleanest first live pipeline because the raw data already exists in OpenClaw artifacts. It is the least ambiguous place to prove ingestion, normalization, freshness, and dashboard contracts.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: RDB has the strongest quantified pain today: suspicious duplicates, known table bloat, and direct operational friction. That is a better near-term target than an abstract full-stack rebuild.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: The temptation here is obvious: BSOP live data, DILESA architecture cleanup, RDB systems work, travel product polish — all true, all too much at once. Sequencing is the real strategy.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: A coherent stack is emerging: BSOP as wrapper, Coda as workflow UI, Supabase only where live sync or outside sharing requires it. The council should explicitly anchor decisions to that architecture.

debate.topic: Cross-critiques — what could fail or distract execution?

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: To the Engineer: a telemetry spine is valuable only if it produces operating decisions, not another analytics toy. Tie it immediately to cost control, delegation quality, and freshness confidence.

highlights.speaker: engineer
highlights.message: To the Strategist: DILESA is the right flagship, but not if we frame it as a giant migration. It needs wave-based architecture work with explicit boundaries, or it becomes an endless refactor.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: To the Strategist and Synthesizer: travel is promising, but it is still a sidecar business case. RDB and AI Operations have better measurable urgency this week.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: To everyone: non-technical staff at RDB are a hard constraint. Any solution that asks them to behave differently before back-office controls are invisible and stable is likely to fail socially, not technically.

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: The common pattern behind every good idea is not “more software”; it is tighter trust loops: canonical records, freshness labels, smaller surfaces, and one owner per metric or queue.

debate.topic: Resolution — what the council actually recommends now

highlights.speaker: synthesizer
highlights.message: Consensus: the first P0 is the live telemetry spine because it is the lowest-risk architectural proving ground. The second P0 is RDB data integrity because the pain is real and current.

highlights.speaker: critic
highlights.message: Consensus with warning: DILESA should begin as a formal program, but only after the first P0 work is moving and only with explicit scope discipline.

highlights.speaker: analyst
highlights.message: Consensus: a weekly executive scoreboard is the mechanism that turns these recommendations into management rhythm across ANSA, DILESA, COAGAN, and RDB.

highlights.speaker: strategist
highlights.message: Consensus: travel remains strategically useful as BSOP’s first shared external surface, but it is P2 because it should follow the more urgent operating leverage plays.

recommendations.title: Build the OpenClaw → BSOP live telemetry spine first
recommendations.rationale: This is the cleanest proving ground: bounded scope, immediate value, and directly aligned with Beto’s need for trustworthy AI cost and activity visibility. It also establishes the ingestion pattern BSOP will reuse for future live modules.
actionItems: Define a normalized schema for sessions, messages, model usage, and freshness metadata
Build a scheduled sync that ingests JSONL/manifests and writes a stable dataset for BSOP
Add visible freshness badges and source labels to AI dashboard, usage, and models pages

recommendations.title: Harden RDB with duplicate detection, canonical orders, and archive policy
recommendations.rationale: RDB has a quantified pain signal right now: duplicate behavior in Waitry POS, a known Coda bottleneck, and clear evidence that the issue is operationally real. Fixing integrity and performance around existing workflows beats building a new POS.
actionItems: Formalize duplicate scoring rules using table, amount, item set, and time-window signals
Create a canonical-order layer plus a reviewed-duplicates queue for ops follow-up
Archive old inbound/raw rows on a schedule so the heaviest Coda tables stop slowing the system

recommendations.title: Launch DILESA as the first formal modernization program
recommendations.rationale: DILESA is the most complex and highest-leverage Coda environment. A formal modernization wave there will clarify where Coda remains the right UI and where external data structures should eventually carry load.
actionItems: List the top god tables and high-friction workflows by business criticality, not by technical curiosity
Draft a tiering policy: stay in Coda, archive in Coda, or sync externally later
Start with the land/anteproyectos/proyectos pipeline so development stages are governed coherently

recommendations.title: Create a weekly executive scoreboard across the four businesses
recommendations.rationale: Beto runs multiple operating contexts. A single weekly scoreboard forces cross-business visibility and makes the council’s output useful in Monday decision-making, not just Sunday reflection.
actionItems: Choose 5–8 executive KPIs total across ANSA, DILESA, COAGAN, and RDB
Define owner and source for each metric, including whether it is manual, scheduled, or live
Publish the first scoreboard even if some metrics are manual placeholders, then tighten automation later

recommendations.title: Use shared trip links and expense settlement as BSOP’s first outside-user surface
recommendations.rationale: Travel is already real, emotionally sticky, and naturally collaborative. It is the safest domain to let other people touch BSOP, proving shareable UX and selective Supabase use without risking core business operations.
actionItems: Finish tabs/export polish on trip detail and validate the shared-link flow on actual phones
Add per-person balances, category summaries, and FX transparency to the expense tracker
Use Taos as a live test for itinerary visibility and shared planning behavior
